channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: AI rollout, war impacts to drive specialty innovation in greater China: Markel’s Chelsea Jiang
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aon hands promotions to Edwin Lee, Jamine Figueroa
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Typhoon Bavi slams eastern China, forces millions to evacuate
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Ripe expands embedded golf insurance through Scottish Golf and Affordable Golf partnerships
item.description: The post Ripe expands embedded golf insurance through Scottish Golf and Affordable Golf partnerships appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Golf Care, part of digital MGA Ripe Thinking , has expanded its UK distribution through two partnerships with Scottish Golf and golf equipment retailer Affordable Golf. Separately, Golf Care has launched what it describes as the UK’s first embedded insurance solution for golf retail with Affordable Golf.

item.title: Allianz Malaysia launches multi-year home insurance plan
item.description: Allianz Malaysia launched a multi-year version of its Smart Home Cover policy, offering three years of home insurance with a single upfront payment. With this campaign, the Smart Home Cover Multi-Year Plan not only ensures long-term protection for your home but also rewards our customers with an exclusive gift.” – Sean Wang, Chief Executive Officer of Allianz General. Customers choosing the plan receive lower premiums, avoid annual renewals, and get free reinstatement for the first two valid claims under the HouseOwner or HouseHolder sections.

item.title: Steadfast deal – devil is in the discount
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Buenas Fuentes: Galicia y Rappi se unen en Argentina
item.description: Galicia y Rappi impulsan finanzas embebidas en Argentina Galicia y Rappi integrarán soluciones de pago, crédito y bancarización dentro de la plataforma de delivery en Argentina. La alianza ofrecerá herramientas financieras a más de 15.000 comercios y 45.000 repartidores, ampliando la distribución de servicios mediante finanzas embebidas. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana.

item.title: Carmakers Broadly Win Round One in Massive UK Suits Over Diesel Emissions
item.description: Some of the world’s biggest carmakers on Friday largely won the first stage of a major legal battle over claims that their diesel vehicles were fitted with unlawful “defeat devices” to curtail emissions controls. In a summary of her judgment, Judge Sara Cockerill said she had “rejected most of the principal allegations advanced against the manufacturers whose vehicles were examined at trial.” She said that a defeat device under emissions regulation only covered “devices which operate with the intentional and/or impermissible purpose of causing the (emissions control system) to operate differently when it senses the test cycle.” The case centered on 20 sample vehicles made by Mercedes-Benz, Ford, Nissan, Renault and the Stellantis-owned brands Peugeot and Citroen. Cockerill did make some adverse findings, including over a coolant temperature device used in some Mercedes cars that was removed in a December 2015 update.

item.title: UK to Regulate Cloud Service Providers Microsoft, Google And Others to Protect Financial Stability
item.description: Britain has designated cloud service providers Microsoft, Google, Amazon and Oracle as critical third-party suppliers to its financial sector, bringing them under direct regulatory oversight. The government designated Microsoft Ireland Operations Ltd, Google Cloud EMEA Ltd, Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL, and Oracle Corporation UK Ltd as critical third parties, effective July 13. A Google Cloud spokesperson said: “With effective implementation and meaningful industry engagement, this new Critical Third Party framework can enhance the long-term resilience of the UK’s financial ecosystem and increase understanding, transparency, and trust between all parties.” (Reporting by Seers and Muvija M; Editing by William James and Mark Potter) Get the latest insurance news sent straight to your inbox.

item.title: Nearly 463,000 U.S. Kia Owners Warned to Park Outside Over Fire Risks
item.description: National Highway Traffic Safety Administration urged owners of 463,000 Kia crossover SUVs to park outdoors because of fire risks until new recall repairs are completed. The agency also urged owners to park outside after the initial recall in 2024. from the 2020 through 2024 model years because the front power seat motor may overheat, potentially causing a fire.

item.title: Month-long Speeding Awareness Campaign Aims to Reduce Fatalities
item.description: A new National Highway Traffic Safety Administration campaign highlights the dangers of speeding, a factor in nearly one-third of traffic deaths annually. In 2024, 11,288 people were killed in speeding-related crashes, accounting for 29% of all traffic fatalities, and an estimated 316,757 people were also injured. New preliminary estimates for 2025 project an 11% decline in speeding-related fatalities with an estimated 10,035 deaths.

item.title: Four Insurers Join Ward’s Annual Top 50 P/C Ranking for 2026
item.description: Property/casualty insurers have started to broadcast the fact that they earned spots on Ward’s list of 50 top-performing insurance companies for 2026, but only one was named for 36 straight years—RLI Corp. Ward, which provides benchmarking and best practices studies for the insurance industry, annually analyzes the financial performance for P/C and life insurers by reviewing five-year average measures of growth and profit, compiling lists of the top 50 carriers in each segment since 1991. According to Ward, a unit of Aon, the 50 P/C top performers for 2026 produced a 13.1 % statutory return on average equity from 2021 to 2025, compared to 8.6% for the P/C industry overall.

item.title: Logbook Lending Limited enters administration
item.description: On 1 July 2026, Logbook Lending Limited (trading as AFPremier.co.uk, pawnmy.co.uk, LBL Asset Finance, Log Book Loans 247) entered administration. Logbook Lending Limited provided lending secured on vehicles (known as logbook loans or bill of sale agreements), as well as pawnbroking. If you believe you have a claim against Logbook Lending Limited, or have any questions about your loan, the firm and Joint Administrators can be contacted on: Firm contact number: 020 8731 2557 Firm email: [email protected] Joint Administrators email: [email protected] Customers who are struggling financially can get free and impartial guidance from MoneyHelper .

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: A Pair Of Billion-Dollar Deals For Cyber And AI Infrastructure Lead
item.description: AI once again dominated venture funding this week, claiming five of the 10 largest announced rounds, including a pair of billion-dollar financings for AI infrastructure and cybersecurity that led the pack. This is a weekly feature that runs down the week’s top 10 announced funding rounds in the U.S. Check out last week’s biggest funding deal roundup here .

item.title: Porch secures debut $100m Harbor Crest Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond at lower pricing
item.description: Porch Group, the US homeowner’s specialist insurer has now priced its debut catastrophe bond, securing the targeted $100 million in multi-peril collateralized catastrophe reinsurance through the Harbor Crest Re Ltd. As a result, Porch Group has managed to successfully secure its targeted multi-peril collateralized catastrophe reinsurance limit at lower than anticipated pricing through its debut cat bond. In our first update on this deal, we reported that the size target had not been changed from the initial $100 million, however, Porch was looking to capitalise on investor appetites for risk by lowering the pricing of the notes on this Harbor Crest Re 2026-1 issuance.

item.title: Arch Insurance NA launches new US-based transactional liability team
item.description: Arch Insurance North America, part of Arch Capital Group Ltd., has expanded its Transactional Risk Insurance Product capabilities through the launch of a new U.S.-based transactional liability team. Anne Hardner, Executive Vice President, Executive Assurance for Arch Insurance, said, “Arch has been an active participant in the transactional risk market for more than 15 years through MGA and MGU partners and a variety of channels across North America, Bermuda and the United Kingdom. Their experience will help accelerate our growth while maintaining the disciplined approach that defines Arch.” Carson added, “Transactional risk insurance has become an important part of today’s M&A landscape, and Arch is uniquely positioned to deliver the financial strength, underwriting discipline and long-term commitment that brokers and clients increasingly value.

item.title: Lockton appoints Fiona Terry to lead Canadian Global Solutions team
item.description: Lockton, a privately held independent insurance brokerage, has announced the appointment of Fiona Terry as Managing Director and National Practice Leader for its Canadian Global Solutions team. The addition of Fiona in Canada helps Canadian multinational corporations face highly complex, multi-jurisdictional risks that require more than just off-the-shelf coverage,” added Michael Lombardi, President of Lockton Global Solutions. “The addition of Fiona to our team is a significant milestone as we build out our specialized advisory capabilities in Canada,” commented Stéphane Lespérance, Chief Executive Officer of Lockton’s Canadian retail business.

item.title: 2023 attachment point shift helps reinsurers outperform industry cat trends: JP Morgan
item.description: Since the turn of the market in 2023, reinsurers have benefited from the favourable economics, enabling them to outperform industry catastrophe trends, according to JP Morgan’s recent ‘Love Actuary’ report. Beyond these significant rate hikes, structural adjustments within reinsurance programmes shifted in favour of reinsurers, marked by a notable average escalation in attachment points of up to 40%, according to the report. The level has increased from ~$80bn in 2020 to ~ $150bn in 2025 driven by trends such as inflation, higher prevalence of catastrophe losses and urbanisation,” JP Morgan highlighted.

item.title: Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. Acquires Kansas-Based Med James
item.description: (RPS), has acquired Overland Park, Kansas-based Med James, Inc. Med James is a managing general agency (MGA) and wholesale insurance broker serving retail agents. Pam Donahue and the Med James team will remain in their current location under the direction of Jacey Norberg, VP-North Central Region for RPS.

item.title: Business Moves: MGA Fusion Specialty Acquired by UK Asset Manager Arrow Global; Arthur J. Gallagher Acquires Canada’s Wilson M. Beck Insurance Services
item.description: MGA Fusion Specialty Acquired by Asset Manager Arrow Global Arrow Global Group (Arrow), a UK-based investment manager specializing in private credit and real estate, announced its subsidiary Arrow Global Insurance (AGI) has acquired Fusion Specialty Group , a specialist managing general agent. AGI was launched in March by Arrow Global Group as a business vertical designed to capture institutional investment opportunities across the insurance value chain. “Arrow Global Insurance is an established specialty insurance platform that combines exceptional underwriting businesses with institutional capital,” commented Zach Lewy, chief executive officer and chief investment officer of Arrow Global Group, in a statement.

item.title: Company Ordered to Remove Thousands of Wind Turbine Blades in Texas
item.description: A Texas district court this week issued a temporary injunction against a wind turbine recycling company that has stockpiled thousands of wind turbine blades at two facilities in Sweetwater. Related: Thousands of Dumped Wind-Turbine Blades Prompt Crackdown in Texas Under the temporary injunction, Global must obtain permits from TCEQ to process the blades at the facilities and then process and remove the blades. The temporary injunction orders Global Fiberglass Solutions to immediately cease accepting shipments of wind turbine blades and to remove all the blades within the next two years.

item.title: Roadzen to Acquire European Car Rental MGA for Approximately US$15 Million to Deploy AI Underwriting
item.description: The post Roadzen to Acquire European Car Rental MGA for Approximately US$15 Million to Deploy AI Underwriting appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Roadzen has agreed to acquire an unnamed European car rental managing general agent for approximately US$15 million, aiming to replace static pricing with real-time, AI-powered underwriting at the point of rental booking. Roadzen has agreed to acquire an unnamed European car rental managing general agent for approximately US$15 million, aiming to replace static pricing with real-time, AI-powered underwriting at the point of rental booking.

item.title: Cybersecurity Training in Insurance Must Keep Pace
item.description: One thing that has become clear is annual cybersecurity training alone is no longer enough to keep pace with such an ambitious moving target like cyber hygiene. Few industries have a greater need for effective cybersecurity training than insurance. While insurance regulations mandate cybersecurity training, checking a regulatory box does not create lasting behavioral change.

item.title: PURE partners with FM
item.description: “We chose the PURE team as our partners as we enter this space because they share the belief that the majority of property loss is preventable through stronger understanding of risk and close collaboration with clients. Commercial property insurer FM has partnered with high-net-worth insurer PURE Insurance to bring FM’s engineering and loss prevention capabilities to high-value homes. The long-term partnership will give PURE members access to FM’s research, engineering, and risk mitigation expertise to help improve property resilience and reduce losses.

item.title: Marsh McLennan Agency launches trustee crime insurance
item.description: Marsh McLennan Agency (MMA) has launched a Trustee Crime Insurance Solution for high-net-worth clients, offering coverage for theft committed by trustees. The company says the offering addresses a gap left by traditional crime insurance policies, which typically cover theft by employees but not trustees. The new product expands MMA’s trust insurance offerings for high-net-worth clients, which also include property and liability coverage.

item.title: Regulators Don’t Want an AI Policy. They Want Receipts.
item.description: The post Regulators Don’t Want an AI Policy. Run the 25-file test Claims leaders do not need to wait for an examination to learn whether they can produce receipts. They began with principles, acceptable-use rules, model inventories, steering committees, and vendor questionnaires.

item.title: MiCA filtra mercado cripto en Europa, ¿qué esperar en LatAm?
item.description: Mientras el reglamento MiCA (Markets in Crypto-Assets) acaba de dejar fuera a la mayoría de los proveedores que buscaban operar en la Unión Europea, actores del sector en Latam advierten que trasladar ese modelo a la región podría elevar las barreras de entrada y frenar el desarrollo de una industria que aún está en construcción. ¿Podría Latinoamérica soportar una regulación cripto tan estricta como la europea? Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca.

item.title: Hormuz Ship Insurance Demand Drops as Owners Get Nervous
item.description: At the higher end of that range, it would cost $6 million to insure an oil tanker worth $100 million while transiting Hormuz, although owners often receive large no-claim discounts that can reduce headline rates. London marine insurers have been seeing fewer inquiries for journeys to transit the Strait of Hormuz and some said the cost of cover had risen, in a sign of growing caution from shipowners as the U.S. Two marine war insurance brokers and two underwriters said they have seen fewer requests for quotes since a ceasefire in the Middle East all but broke down this week.

item.title: Marsh, QBE, Allianz Partners, Aon, Time Machine: 16 APAC insurance people moves of the week
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: UK financial regulators to begin overseeing Critical Third Parties announced by Treasury
item.description: The Bank of England (the Bank), the Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) and the FCA will start overseeing the first critical third parties (CTPs) on Monday 13 July 2026, following designation by the Treasury. Sarah Breeden, deputy governor for financial stability at the Bank, said: 'As critical third parties become increasingly embedded in the operations of financial institutions, they can introduce new forms of systemic risk. For the first time, the three regulators will jointly oversee these CTPs under a new, proportionate regime, focused on the resilience of the critical services they provide to the UK financial sector.

item.title: 5 Interesting Startup Deals You May Have Missed: AI That Dispatches The Plumber, Underground Warfare And Cutting Down Private-Market Paperwork
item.description: This is a monthly column that runs down five interesting startup funding deals that may have flown under the radar. They include a startup that’s simultaneously developing AI models for biology and trying to prevent the threats that stem from those types of advances, a company that says it wants to prevent modern day private markets from the kind of paperwork crisis that shut down Wall Street in the ’60s, and AI agents that can dispatch plumbers and electricians to your door. Our inboxes overflowed with interesting deals in the past month, but we managed to sift through them all to find the five most intriguing ones.

item.title: Insurance capacity constraints for data centres to drive ILS use: S&P
item.description: With capacity constraints likely to limit the insurance industry’s ability to fully insure hyperscale data centre projects, given their scale and complexity, a new report from S&P notes that this will drive greater use of self-insurance through captive insurers, and potentially, alternative capital such as insurance-linked securities (ILS). “We expect capacity constraints to limit the insurance industry’s ability to fully insure hyperscale data center projects as total insurable values reach $20 billion-$30 billion per location. At the same time, S&P also indicated that annual investment in data centres could surpass $300 billion by 2027, highlighting the industry’s explosive growth and the growing insurance requirements associated with large-scale infrastructure development.

item.title: Why data operations are core to casualty reinsurance, rather than a supporting function: Peter McKelvy, Northern Re
item.description: “We felt strongly that getting operations and data right was not a supporting function, but core to running a casualty reinsurance business properly,” McKelvy added. To begin, McKelvy outlined why both operations and data have become a key focus for Northern Re in casualty reinsurance. Given this, Peter McKelvy, Co-Founder of Northern Re, speaks to Artemis to discuss how the company’s platform updates models instantly to eliminate this information lag.

item.title: Existing cyber cat bonds structured to withstand Claude Mythos turbulence: Man Group
item.description: As Anthropic’s advanced cybersecurity AI model, Claude Mythos, continues to generate significant noise across the cyber sector, investment manager Man Group notes that the per-occurrence structures and high attachment points of existing cyber catastrophe bonds should provide meaningful insulation from future turbulence, for when more advanced AI models eventually become widely available. So, what are the implications for cyber security, cyber insurance, and cyber catastrophe (cat) bonds,” Man Group said. In early April, Anthropic announced the successor to its Claude Opus model: Mythos Preview.

item.title: Howden Re appoints Thomas Fitzgerald as Head of Global Client Relations & Vice Chair
item.description: Reinsurance broker Howden Re has appointed Thomas Fitzgerald as Head of Global Client Relations and Vice Chair. In his new role, Fitzgerald will lead global client and reinsurer relations, focusing on client engagement and market innovation. Fitzgerald brings more than 30 years of experience across the global insurance and brokerage industry.

item.title: AI infrastructure investment is reshaping commercial risk pools: Swiss Re
item.description: The global investment boom in AI infrastructure is reshaping commercial risk pools. The rapid construction of increasingly large and complex data centres has triggered a surge in demand for insurance, fuelled by growing investment in artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure, energy systems and semiconductor manufacturing. According to Swiss Re’s Sigma 2026 report, AI hyperscaler capital expenditure is expected to reach $750 billion in 2026, while AI-related investment accounted for nearly 40% of US GDP growth during the first three quarters of 2025.

item.title: Chris Moore returns to OdysseyRe as VP, Senior Property Underwriter
item.description: Odyssey Reinsurance Company (OdysseyRe), the property and casualty reinsurance arm of Odyssey Group Holdings, Inc., a subsidiary of Fairfax Financial Holdings Limited, has appointed Chris Moore as Vice President (VP), Senior Property Underwriter. OdysseyRe commented on the appointment, “Chris had previously served on the property underwriter team in various roles with increasing responsibility from 2008 to 2024. Moore returns to the reinsurer after spending the last two years as a Senior Director of Reinsurance for The Cincinnati Insurance Companies.

item.title: Damaged Manhattan Tower Owner to Reconstruct 15 Floors After Evacuation
item.description: The developer behind the Midtown Manhattan tower that was evacuated over structural concerns Tuesday says it has a plan to fix the issue, including reconstructing 15 floors that were added to the original building as part of its conversion into apartments. Read More: Manhattan Developer of Midtown Tower Dismisses Fears of Collapse Metro Loft is planning more than 1,600 apartments at the site, one of the largest office-to-housing conversions underway in the US. Industry experts said the issues at the damaged Midtown tower may ultimately require more extensive repair.

item.title: Climate Models Become More Precise Even as Political Attacks Sharpen
item.description: As attribution science becomes more precise, some academic experts believe the research will be increasingly used in court to hold companies and governments financially liable for the damage caused by climate change. Models are facing greater political scrutiny more generally. As back-to-back heat waves batter the US and Europe, scientific models linking climate change to extreme weather are increasingly important for policymakers and commercial enterprises like insurers.

item.title: California Real Estate Agent Sentenced in LA Wildfire Gouging Case
item.description: A real estate agent who tried to price gouge a couple who lost their home during the Los Angeles wildfires was sentenced for false advertising. California Attorney General Rob Bonta announced the sentencing of real estate agent Mike Kobeissi for false advertising, which arose arising out of an attempt to price gouge a couple who lost their home in the Eaton Fire. The order prohibited price gouging of wildfire victims.

item.title: Everest appoints Craig Hanrahan as chief commercial officer amid softening insurance market
item.description: The post Everest appoints Craig Hanrahan as chief commercial officer amid softening insurance market appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Everest Group has appointed Craig Hanrahan as global distribution and chief commercial officer, effective August 1, as the global insurer and reinsurer strengthens its commercial leadership at a time when pricing across both insurance and reinsurance markets is becoming more competitive. In his new role, he will oversee Everest’s global distribution strategy and manage key commercial relationships across both its insurance and reinsurance operations.

item.title: Mox and QBE launch annual travel plan in Hong Kong
item.description: Mox Bank has launched the Travel Pick & Go Annual Plan, a fully digital travel insurance product underwritten by QBE Hong Kong , expanding the companies’ exclusive bancassurance partnership. The annual travel plan is the latest product under the companies’ Mox Insure partnership, which previously introduced personal accident and single-trip travel insurance. The annual policy covers unlimited trips over a 12-month period, with a maximum duration of 90 days per trip, and includes medical coverage, 24/7 emergency assistance, travel delay protection, and five add-on benefits at no additional cost.

item.title: Freeway adds California and Alabama franchises
item.description: Freeway Insurance announced the opening of five new franchise locations in California and Alabama as it continues expanding its retail footprint. The new offices are located in Millbrook, Alabama, and Clovis, Brentwood, Pomona, and Galt, California. Freeway, a division of Confie, operates in 28 states and was recognized in Entrepreneur’s 2025 Franchise 500 ranking.

item.title: 3 Technology Must-Haves to Build Distribution Velocity
item.description: The agencies leading the market are building their distribution velocity around three essential technology capabilities within their agency management systems: 1. By reducing repetitive work and accelerating everyday process, AI helps agencies build Distribution Velocity without increasing staff or sacrificing service quality. Forward-thinking agencies are shifting their focus from simply managing policies to accelerating what Vertafore calls Distribution Velocity .

item.title: Bruselas aprueba la adquisición de Beazley por Zurich
item.description: La Comisión Europea ha aprobado, en el marco del Reglamento de Concentraciones de la UE y mediante el procedimiento simplificado de revisión, la adquisición del control exclusivo de Beazley por parte de Zurich. La conclusión ha sido que la operación no plantea problemas de competencia dado el limitado posicionamiento de mercado de ambas compañías.

item.title: El Senado aprueba la pasarela de mutualistas al RETA con la exclusión de los ya jubilados
item.description: El Senado ha aprobado el dictamen de la proposición de ley que modifica el Real Decreto Legislativo 8/2015, texto refundido de la Ley General de la Seguridad Social, en lo relativo a las mutualidades alternativas reguladas en sus disposiciones adicionales 18.ª y 19.ª.

item.title: Allianz moviliza a 1.000 empleados por el impacto social
item.description: El voluntariado corporativo está evolucionando y buena cuenta de ello se observa en Allianz España: durante el primer semestre de 2026 casi 1.000 empleados de la aseguradora participaron en iniciativas de impacto social y medioambiental, dedicando más de 3.300 horas de voluntariado, un 21,2% más que en el mismo periodo del año anterior.

item.title: El Niño Likely Strongest in 75 Years, Forecasters Say
item.description: The weather-roiling El Niño that emerged across the Pacific last month has continued to build and will likely be one of the strongest in more than 75 years, the U.S. Related: ‘Snow Globe’ Effect, Quiet Cat Years, Super El Niño: Carriers Prepping for a Raucous Second Half “Even the strongest El Niño events do not lead to the typical impact everywhere, but stronger events can more significantly tilt the odds in favor of expected outcomes,” the Climate Prediction Center said. Sea surface temperatures of 1C (1.8F) or more above normal, the hallmark of the phenomenon, have spread across the central and eastern equatorial Pacific, and there’s an 81% chance it will become a very strong El Niño and rank among the “largest events in the historical record going back to 1950,” the agency said in its monthly forecast.

item.title: CSU Lowers Atlantic Hurricane Forecast to ‘Well Below Normal’
item.description: Thanks to a strengthening El Niño , the rest of the Atlantic hurricane season will be well below normal, said researchers at Colorado State University. Related: Weathering NOAA’s ‘Below Normal Atlantic Hurricane Season’ And A Super El Niño Should CSU’s forecast hold up, 2026 would be the quietest season for hurricanes since 2015, when there were four hurricanes and two major hurricanes. The CSU team said it expects El Niño to “dominate the tropical circulation during the peak of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season.” CSU’s original forecast in April called for a below-average season of 13 named storms, six hurricanes and two major hurricanes.

item.title: Ex-Banker Convicted in Deadly Car Crash Seeks Ferrari Records
item.description: An ex-Deutsche Bank AG banker imprisoned over a fatal car crash in Hong Kong is seeking to clear his name in a UK court by forcing Ferrari to hand over internal documents he claims could prove the vehicle was unsafe. Robert Ebert, Deutsche Bank’s former head of Asia-Pacific equities in Hong Kong, was driving a Ferrari 458 Spider in 2015 when he lost control of the car and hit a security guard, who later died. Ebert was convicted of causing death by dangerous driving and imprisoned for 14 months, according to court documents filed for a London hearing on Tuesday.
